Description.Name: Thunder Hawk
Description.Details: The Thunder Hawk was built by Norse-Storm BattleMechs in 2771, during the final six years of the SLDF's drive to retake the Terran Hegemony. The Thunder Hawk epitomizes the doctrine of firepower over maneuverability with its heavy weapons arsenal and low top speed. Taken out of production after the end of the Amaris Civil War, Thunder Hawk production began again during the mid-3050s. Norse-Storm attempted, in the wake of the Clan Invasion, to make it their flagship design, though trouble manufacturing enough 'Mechs and parts to meet demand weakened its market share over decades afterwards. The Thunder Hawk rumbles through the battlefield at a maximum speed of 54 km/h. Despite the low speed, the Thunder Hawk is powered by a light weight Vlar 300 XL engine that weighs half as much as a standard fusion engine and allows the 'Mech to carry a heavier than normal weapons payload. The 'Mech is protected by nineteen and a half tons of armor, giving it more armor than the venerable Atlas. Additionally, because the Thunder Hawk has very little heat buildup from its weapons, it carries only ten heat sinks. The Thunder Hawk is armed to engage an enemy at stand-off ranges when facing any opposition. The weapons that enable the Thunder Hawk to achieve this are a trio of Norse-Storm Model 7D Gauss rifles fed by six tons of ammo split evenly across the torso locations. When combined, these main guns can cripple or outright destroy most lighter 'Mechs in only a few short salvos. To defend itself either when an enemy closes to short range or its Gauss ammo runs dry, the Thunder Hawk has an impressive array of four Defiance B3M medium lasers: Two in the left arm, one in the right, and one in the head.

YangsThoughts: Three full Gauss rifles on a single mech, this thing is a monster of long range firepower. I bet we could make it crazy if we used Clan-spec rifles, we might even be able to get 4 of them on there.
